content:
Mission

Refactor the Evolution One CMS codebase page by page (3 pages at a time, if possible use 3 agents, 1 per page) ensuring strict adherence to all guidelines in .instructions/ and .claude/skills/. Deeply analyze every component, type, folder, helper, and utility — questioning placement, redundancy, cleanliness, security, and modularity.

Source of Truth — Must Follow Exactly

Core Rules (.instructions/rules/)

File	Key Requirements

type-safety.md	Zero any, type over interface, .lean<T>() mandatory, no Record<string,unknown>, explicit return types, no comments in type files

nextjs-rules.md	Direct React imports, lean page.tsx wrappers (<150 lines), component section comments with =====, custom hook structure (13 sections), skeleton loaders mandatory, financial color coding

naming-conventions.md	[PageName]PageContent, [PageName][Section][Component], Props suffix, handle/fetch/format/calculate/map prefixes, no single-letter vars, use-case-based file names

mongoose-query-typing.md	.lean<T>(), .lean<T>().cursor().toArray(), .aggregate<T>(), types from shared/types/

http-https-cookie-rules.md	Conditional secure cookie flag via isSecureContext(), never hardcode secure: true

currency-display.md	Never hardcode $, use formatCurrencyWithCodeString(amount, displayCurrency), [Sign][CurrencyCode] [Amount] format

application-context.md	Gaming day 8AM-8AM Trinidad, movement delta method, net gross = gross - jackpot, skeleton loaders match exact layout

guidlines.md	API route structure (JSDoc, step comments, helper extraction <30 lines, <500 lines/routes), backend standards

authorisation.md	RBAC hierarchy, reviewer scale (1 - multiplier), licencee/location filtering via getUserLocationFilter()

Skills (.claude/skills/) — Apply When Relevant

api-route-structure — API route patterns

backend-standards — Mongoose, licencee filtering, aggregation

code-style-eslint — ESLint zero-tolerance, file-level docs

database-query-optimization — .cursor(), direct location field, eliminate N+1, gaming day offsets

file-organization — Directory structure decisions

gaming-day-offset-system — 8AM offset, per-location, custom dates use it

licencee-access-control — Multi-tenant, role-based, session invalidation

page-component-structure — Thin wrappers, section comments, JSX formatting

react-typescript-rules — React imports, Zustand, type organization

skeleton-loaders — Content-specific, no generic spinners, exact layout match

Refactoring Protocol — Per Page (3 at a Time)

Phase

For Each Page, Do Deep Analysis Of:

1. Page Structure (app/[page]/page.tsx)

 Thin wrapper (<150 lines) with ProtectedRoute + PageErrorBoundary + PageContent

 File-level JSDoc with @module and features list

 Section comments: Hooks & State → Computed → Handlers → Effects → Return

 Custom hooks extracted for complex state/logic

 Data fetching in lib/helpers/ not inline

 selectedLicencee in useEffect deps (critical!)

2. Page Content Component (components/[Page]/[PageName]PageContent.tsx)

 Section comments with domain-specific headers (not generic)

 State grouped by concern (Selection, Form, Processing, UI)

 useMemo for expensive computations

 useCallback for handlers

 No Render Logic header — clean transition to return

 Responsive: hidden md:block / md:hidden

 Content-specific skeleton loader imported from components/ui/skeletons/

3. All Child Components (components/[Page]/...)

 Naming: [PageName][Section][ComponentName] + Props suffix

 Prop types in lib/types/components.ts or co-located if unique

 Extracted to subfolders: tabs/, modals/, forms/, tables/, cards/, sections/, details/, common/, layouts/, mobile/, skeletons/

 JSX comments for major sections: /* ===== Header ===== */

 No single-letter variables anywhere

 Financial colors via getFinancialColorClass / getGrossColorClass

 Skeleton loaders match exact final layout

4. Custom Hooks (lib/hooks/[feature]/use[HookName].ts)

 File-level JSDoc with architecture overview

 Strict section order: External Deps → Types → Helpers → Hook → Store State → Local State (grouped) → Form Bindings → Computed → Debounced → Refs → Effects → Handlers (categorized) → Return

 State grouped by concern with comments

 Handlers grouped by category with comments

 useDebounce for search inputs

 Refs for callback persistence

5. API Routes (app/api/[route]/route.ts)

 File-level JSDoc with @module

 Step-by-step comments with ===== separators, numbered steps

 Flow documented in handler JSDoc

 Helpers >30 lines extracted to app/api/lib/helpers/[feature].ts

 Route <500 lines, helper <600 lines

 .lean<T>() on all queries, .cursor() for Meters

 Licencee/location filtering via getUserLocationFilter()

 Reviewer scale via getReviewerScale() from app/api/lib/utils/reviewerScale.ts

 Parameter guards at function start

 Critical operation result checking with logging

 Performance tracking (Date.now())

6. Types — Placement & Quality

 Shared (shared/types/): Used by FE + BE (models, DTOs, entities, api, analytics, auth, common)

 Frontend (lib/types/): Components, hooks, UI-only

 Backend (app/api/lib/types/): API-only, DB operations

 type not interface

 No comments in type files

 Explicit generics on .lean<T>(), .aggregate<T>()

 No Record<string,unknown> — use Pick, Partial, composed types

 String literal unions over enums

7. Helpers/Utils — Placement & Quality

 Backend (app/api/lib/helpers/[feature].ts): Business logic, DB ops

 Frontend (lib/helpers/[feature].ts): API calls, data transformation

 Shared (lib/utils/[domain]/[purpose].ts): Math, formatting, movement calc

 File names = use case (mapping.ts, normalization.ts, validation.ts, totals.ts, colors.ts)

 No frontendCalculation.ts / clientUtils.ts prefixes

 Group in subfolder if 3+ related files, else merge if <100 lines each

 Explicit return types, parameter guards, JSDoc per function

8. Security & Access Control

 getUserLocationFilter() on ALL location/machine-scoped queries

 Both licencee and licencee query params supported

 Reviewer scale applied to ALL financial returns

 Session invalidation on permission changes (sessionVersion++)

 Conditional cookie secure via isSecureContext(request)

 No direct db.collection() — Mongoose models only

 findOne({ _id }) not findById()

9. Performance

 Meters aggregations: .cursor({ batchSize: 1000 }) not .exec()

 Direct location field on Meters, no $lookup

 Batch queries, eliminate N+1

 Gaming day offset: global range + in-memory filter

 useMemo/useCallback for expensive renders

 Debounced search inputs

10. Redundancy & Cleanliness Audit

 Delete unused imports/exports (verify with grep first)

 Consolidate duplicate types — single source in shared/types/

 Remove redundant comments (keep WHY, delete WHAT)

 Merge small related files (<100 lines) if improves discoverability

 Extract reusable UI to components/ui/

 Extract reusable logic to hooks/helpers

Execution Workflow

FOR each batch of 2 pages:

  1. READ page.tsx + PageContent + all child components

  2. READ all hooks used by page

  3. READ all API routes called by page

  4. READ all helpers/utils/types imported

  5. ANALYZE against every checklist item above

  6. DOCUMENT findings: what's wrong, what to move, what to extract, what to delete

  7. PRESENT plan for approval

  8. EXECUTE refactoring (one file at a time, verify after each)

  9. RUN `bun run check` — must pass

  10. PROCEED to next 2 pages

Output Format Per Batch

## Batch N: [Page A] + [Page B]

### Findings — [Page A]

| Category | File | Issue | Severity | Fix |

|----------|------|-------|----------|-----|

| Structure | page.tsx | 280 lines, logic inline | High | Extract to PageContent + hooks |

| Types | Component.tsx | `interface` used, `Record<string,unknown>` | High | Convert to `type`, compose proper types |

| Security | API route | No licencee filter | Critical | Add `getUserLocationFilter()` |

| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

### Findings — [Page B]

| ... |

### Proposed Actions

1. Move X to shared/types

2. Extract Y to lib/helpers

3. Delete Z (unused, verified)

4. Rename files to use-case naming

5. Add skeleton loaders for A, B, C

...etc.

### Refactored Files (after execution)

- List of every file touched with summary of changes

Pages Priority Order (Suggested)

Dashboard (app/page.tsx) + Reports (app/reports/page.tsx)

Machines/Cabinets (app/cabinets/page.tsx) + Locations (app/locations/page.tsx)

Collection Report (app/collection-report/page.tsx) + Collection Report Details (app/collection-report/report/[id]/page.tsx)

Vault (app/vault/page.tsx) + Vault Transactions (app/vault/transactions/page.tsx)

Administration (app/administration/page.tsx) + Members (app/members/page.tsx)

Bill Validator (app/bill-validator/page.tsx) + Analytics (app/analytics/page.tsx)

Configuration (app/configuration/page.tsx) + Firmware (app/firmware/page.tsx)

Auth pages (login, recovery, 2FA) + Settings/Profile

Quality Gates — Must Pass Before Next Batch and let me review before we move to the next 3 pages

bun run check        # TypeScript + ESLint — 0 errors

bun run build        # Production build succeeds

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

attachment.content:
- api-route-structure: Structure API routes with step-by-step comments, JSDoc, helper extraction, and proper organization.
- backend-standards: Mongoose models over direct collections, findOne() not findById(), licencee filtering, aggregation patterns.
- code-style-eslint: Never ignore ESLint violations, run lint regularly, file-level documentation, remove redundant comments.
- database-query-optimization: Use .cursor() for Meters, direct location field access, eliminate N+1 patterns, handle gaming day offsets.
- file-organization: Directory structure for shared code, frontend, backend, components, and utilities across the application.
- gaming-day-offset-system: 8 AM to 8 AM business day (Trinidad UTC-4), gaming day calculations, custom date handling, per-location offsets.
- licencee-access-control: Multi-tenant access control, role-based permissions, location filtering, session invalidation on permission changes.
- local-app-testing: Logging in and manually testing the Evolution One CMS in a real browser on localhost. Use when driving the app via the browser (Claude in Chrome) to verify a change, test a flow, or reproduce a bug. Contains the dev login credentials.
- page-component-structure: page.tsx thin wrappers, component organization with section comments, and JSX formatting patterns.
- react-typescript-rules: React imports, TypeScript discipline, state management patterns, and type organization rules.
- skeleton-loaders: Content-specific skeleton loaders that exactly match real content layout. NEVER use generic spinners or "Loading..." text.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
I'll start with **Batch 1: Dashboard + Reports** per your priority order. Since you want deep per-page analysis and a plan to review before any execution, I'll run analysis agents (one per page, read-only) against the full checklist, then synthesize findings for your approval. No changes will be made until you approve.

Let me first confirm the exact page entry points, then dispatch the agents.

1	/**
2	 * Dashboard Page
3	 *
4	 * Root page wrapper that renders the main dashboard with financial metrics,
5	 * charts, and key performance indicators. Handles authentication via ProtectedRoute,
6	 * maintenance mode checks, and error boundary wrapping.
7	 */
8	
9	import DashboardPageContent from '@/components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardPageContent';
10	import ProtectedRoute from '@/components/shared/auth/ProtectedRoute';
11	import MaintenanceWrapper from '@/components/shared/maintenance/MaintenanceWrapper';
12	import PageErrorBoundary from '@/components/shared/ui/errors/PageErrorBoundary';
13	export default function DashboardPage() {
14	  return (
15	    <ProtectedRoute requiredPage="dashboard">
16	      <MaintenanceWrapper pageKey="dashboard">
17	        <PageErrorBoundary>
18	          <DashboardPageContent />
19	        </PageErrorBoundary>
20	      </MaintenanceWrapper>
21	    </ProtectedRoute>
22	  );
23	}
24

4	
5	// Components

7	import { ReportsPageSkeleton } from '@/components/shared/ui/skeletons/ReportsSkeletons';
8	import ProtectedRoute from '@/components/shared/auth/ProtectedRoute';
9	import MaintenanceWrapper from '@/components/shared/maintenance/MaintenanceWrapper';

12	 * Reports Page

14	 *

16	 * - Multi-tab interface (Locations, Machines, Meters)
17	 * - Role-based access control and permissions
18	 * - Real-time data updates and filtering
19	 * - Responsive design for desktop and mobile

22	 */

27	        <Suspense fallback={<ReportsPageSkeleton />}>
28	          <ReportsPageContent />
29	        </Suspense>
30	      </MaintenanceWrapper>
31	    </ProtectedRoute>

content.text: Both wrappers are already thin. Now I'll dispatch two read-only analysis agents in parallel — one per page — to deeply audit the full component/hook/API/type/helper tree for each against your checklist. They will only report findings, not edit.

input.prompt:
You are doing a READ-ONLY deep refactoring audit of the Dashboard page of the Evolution One CMS (Next.js App Router, MongoDB/Mongoose, TypeScript). DO NOT edit any files. Only read and report.

Entry point: `app/page.tsx` → renders `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardPageContent.tsx`.

Trace and read EVERYTHING reachable from the Dashboard page:
1. `DashboardPageContent.tsx` and ALL child components under `components/CMS/dashboard/`.
2. ALL custom hooks the page/components use (lib/hooks/...).
3. ALL API routes the dashboard calls (search app/api for the fetched endpoints).
4. ALL helpers/utils/types imported (lib/helpers, lib/utils, app/api/lib/helpers, shared/types, lib/types, app/api/lib/types).

Audit against these project rules (from CLAUDE.md and .instructions/.claude/skills):
- TYPE SAFETY: zero `any`, `type` not `interface`, `.lean<T>()` mandatory on all Mongoose queries, no `Record<string,unknown>` for domain data, explicit return types, NO comments in type files.
- NAMING: `[PageName]PageContent`, `[PageName][Section][Component]`, `Props` suffix, handle/fetch/format/calculate/map prefixes, no single-letter vars, use-case-based file names.
- MONGOOSE: `.lean<T>()`, `.aggregate<T>()`, `Meters.aggregate().cursor({batchSize:1000})` NOT `.exec()`, types from shared/types, use `location` field directly (no $lookup), no findById, findOne({_id}).
- API ROUTES: file-level JSDoc @module, numbered STEP comments with ===== separators, withApiAuth wrapper, helper extraction >30 lines, route <500 lines / helper <600 lines, performance tracking Date.now(), getUserLocationFilter() for licencee/location filtering (support both `licencee` spellings), getReviewerScale() applied to all financial returns.
- GAMING DAY: 8AM-8AM Trinidad offset via lib/utils/gamingDayRange.ts, global range + in-memory filter.
- CURRENCY: never hardcode `$`, use formatCurrencyWithCodeString(amount, displayCurrency), pass ?currency= to APIs.
- REACT/NEXT: direct React imports (no React namespace), section comments with `// =====`, skeleton loaders matching exact layout (no spinners), financial color via getFinancialColorClass/getGrossColorClass, useMemo/useCallback.
- HOOK STRUCTURE: 13-section order (External Deps→Types→Helpers→Hook→Store→Local State grouped→Form→Computed→Debounced→Refs→Effects→Handlers grouped→Return).
- FILE ORG / placement: shared/types vs lib/types vs app/api/lib/types; lib/helpers vs app/api/lib/helpers; redundancy/duplication; files >limits.
- SECURITY: location filter on all scoped queries, reviewer scale, session invalidation, conditional cookie secure.
- PERFORMANCE: cursor, N+1, batch queries.
- REDUNDANCY: unused imports/exports/types, duplicate types, dead code, files that should be merged/split.

For each issue found, capture: Category | File (with path) | Issue | Severity (Critical/High/Med/Low) | Concrete Fix.

content.text:
Confirmed: `DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` is imported nowhere (only self-match) — it's dead code. I now have everything needed for the report.

## Dashboard Findings

| Category | File | Issue | Severity | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Redundancy / Dead code | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` | Entire component is unused — imported nowhere (grep confirms only self-reference). It still renders a placeholder `"Pie chart would render here"`, has its own duplicate `TopPerformingItem` type, and calls a separate `/api/metrics/top-performing` endpoint the live dashboard doesn't use. | High | Delete the file. If the `/api/metrics/top-performing` route is only used by this dead component + e2e mocks, flag that route for review too. |
| Type Safety | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` (L5-14) | Local `TopPerformingItem` type duplicates and conflicts with `lib/types` `TopPerformingItem`; uses index signature `[key: string]: unknown` for domain data. Also `catch (err: unknown)` annotates the catch param (forbidden). | Med | Removed with the file. If kept, import the shared type and drop the annotation. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L143-145), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L127-129) | `gamingLocations?.some((loc: Record<string, unknown>) => loc.includeJackpot)` casts domain data to `Record<string,unknown>`. The shared `locations` type has no `includeJackpot` field, so the cast hides a type gap. | Med | Add `includeJackpot?: boolean` to the `locations` element type in `lib/types/index.ts` and type the callback param as that element. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L48-61), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L43-56) | `selectedMachine`/`selectedLocation` inline object types are duplicated verbatim across both layouts (and overlap with modal prop types). | Low | Extract a shared `SelectedMachinePreview` / `SelectedLocationPreview` type to `lib/types/components`. |
| Mongoose Typing | `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/analytics.ts` (L230, L302) | Both `Machine.aggregate([...])` calls use no generic (`Machine.aggregate<T>`) and end in `.exec()`. CLAUDE.md mandates `.aggregate<T>()` with types from `shared/types`. (Meters-cursor rule does not apply here since it's `Machine`, and `$lookup` to gaminglocations is acceptable for Machine, not Meters.) | Med | Add explicit aggregate generics typed from shared types; results are bounded (grouped to one doc) so `.exec()` is acceptable but should still be typed. |
| API Route Structure | `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts` | Does NOT use the mandatory `withApiAuth` wrapper — auth is done manually via `getUserFromServer`/`getUserAccessibleLicenceesFromToken`. Other invariants (numbered STEP comments, JSDoc, perf tracking) are present, but wrapper is required by CLAUDE.md §Technical Standards 1. | High | Refactor to `withApiAuth(req, async ({ user, userRoles }) => {...})`. |
| Reviewer Scale (Security) | `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts` | Returns financial totals (`totalDrop`, `totalCancelledCredits`, `totalGross` via `getMachineStatsForAnalytics`) with NO `getReviewerScale` applied. The dashboard machine-status widget only shows counts, but the financial fields leak unscaled. By contrast `locationAggregation` correctly applies `getMoneyInScale`/`getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale`. | High | Apply reviewer scaling to the financial fields before returning, or strip the financial fields from this endpoint's response since the dashboard only consumes counts. |
| Reviewer Scale consistency | `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts` (L390-427) | Uses split `getMoneyInScale`/`getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale` rather than `getReviewerScale` named in CLAUDE.md §5. Likely an intentional newer two-multiplier model, but worth confirming it's the sanctioned helper. | Low | Confirm `reviewerScale.ts` exports these as the canonical path; update CLAUDE.md if so. |
| Currency (CRITICAL rule) | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx` (L657-685) | Y-axis and Tooltip formatters use raw `toLocaleString`/manual `K/M/B/T` formatting with NO currency code — violates "tooltip formatters must also use `formatCurrencyWithCodeString`". Chart shows bare numbers, no `displayCurrency`. | Med | Inject `displayCurrency` via `useCurrencyFormat()` and format the tooltip value with `formatCurrencyWithCodeString`. |
| Gaming Day Offset | `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingCabinetModal.tsx` (L112), `TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx` (L117) | Hardcode gaming-day offset `8` into `getGamingDayRangeForPeriod('Custom', 8, ...)` instead of using the store's `gameDayOffset` (which the store already tracks and defaults to 8, configurable per location). | Med | Pass `gameDayOffset` from `useDashBoardStore()` instead of literal `8`. |
| React/Component Structure | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L71-79), `DashboardTopPerformingSection.tsx` (L48-56) | `NoDataMessage` is a component defined *inside* the render body (re-created every render); duplicated across both files. | Low | Hoist to a shared `components/shared/ui` component. |
| Skeleton rule | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L216-219), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L192-195) | Map-loading state uses an inline ad-hoc `animate-pulse` div rather than a named skeleton in `skeletons/`. Rule requires content-matching named skeletons, no generic pulse blocks. | Low | Extract a `MapPreviewSkeleton`. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L193-201), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L177-185), and 4 `<select onChange>` casts | Repeated inline union literal `'hourly' | 'minute' | 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly'` (the `ChartGranularity` type) re-typed by hand in ~6 places plus `as` casts on select values. | Low | Use the exported `ChartGranularity` type everywhere instead of re-spelling the union. |
| Naming | `lib/types/index.ts` (L14, L86) | Type names `dashboardData` and `locations` are lowercase (should be PascalCase `DashboardData`, `Locations`/`GamingLocations`). Widely used across the dashboard tree. | Low | Rename with codemod; low priority due to blast radius. |
| Dead state / props | `DashboardPageContent.tsx` (L487, L530) | `setRefreshing={() => {}}` passed as no-op to both layouts; layouts never use it meaningfully. `prevTotals` effect (L428-438) updates a ref that is never read. | Low | Remove the unused `setRefreshing` prop and the dead `prevTotals` tracking effect. |
| Performance / complexity | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx` (809 lines) | Single component does sorting, hourly/weekly/monthly aggregation, gap-filtering, axis formatting — all inline in the render body with no `useMemo`. Heavy O(n) passes recompute every render. Also exceeds reasonable component size. | Med | Extract the aggregation/transform pipeline into a memoized helper (`lib/helpers/dashboard/chartTransform.ts`) and wrap in `useMemo` keyed on `chartData`/`granularity`/`selectedMetrics`. |
| Redundancy | `TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx` (L184-497) | ~300-line inline `fetchData` effect duplicating trend-transform logic that also exists on the location details page (per its own comments "same as location details page"). Should be a shared helper. | Med | Extract to `lib/helpers/locations` shared fetch+transform used by both modal and details page. |
| Error Handling | `lib/utils/metrics.ts` (L81-87) `switchFilter` | Swallows all errors (logs only) including real failures, so the dashboard chart silently shows empty on server errors with no toast. | Low | Distinguish abort vs real error and surface via `showErrorNotification`, mirroring `fetchDashboardTotals`. |
| Dead branch | `DashboardChart.tsx` (L541-547) | `XAxis dataKey` ternary `granularity === 'weekly' || 'monthly' ? 'day' : 'day'` — both branches return `'day'`. | Low | Simplify to `dataKey={isHourlyChart ? 'time' : 'day'}`. |

## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)

1. **Delete dead `DashboardTopPerforming.tsx`** (and assess whether `/api/metrics/top-performing` route is now orphaned — only e2e mocks + this dead file reference it).
2. **Fix `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts`**: wrap in `withApiAuth`, and apply reviewer scaling to (or remove) the financial fields in the response. (Critical: security + structure invariants.)
3. **Add currency formatting to `DashboardChart.tsx`** Y-axis/tooltip via `formatCurrencyWithCodeString` + `displayCurrency`.
4. **Replace hardcoded gaming-day offset `8`** in both preview modals with the store's `gameDayOffset`.
5. **Add aggregate generics** to the two `Machine.aggregate` calls in `analytics.ts`.
6. **Add `includeJackpot?: boolean` to the `locations` type** and drop the `Record<string,unknown>` casts in both layouts.
7. **Extract `DashboardChart` transform pipeline** into a memoized helper; wrap heavy computations in `useMemo`.
8. **Extract the location-trends fetch/transform** out of `TopPerformingLocationModal` into a shared helper.
9. **Hoist shared `NoDataMessage`**, add `MapPreviewSkeleton`, and de-duplicate the `SelectedMachine`/`SelectedLocation` types.
10. **Cleanup**: remove no-op `setRefreshing` props, dead `prevTotals` effect, the `dataKey` no-op ternary; use `ChartGranularity` type instead of inline unions; surface real errors in `switchFilter`.

## Files in the Dashboard tree (read)

Components:
- `app/page.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardPageContent.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardMobileLayout.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` (dead)
- `components/CMS/dashboard/sections/DashboardTopPerformingSection.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingCabinetModal.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx`

Hooks / store:
- `lib/hooks/data/useDashboardFilters.ts`
- `lib/hooks/data/useDashboardRefresh.ts`
- `lib/store/dashboardStore.ts`

Helpers / utils:
- `lib/helpers/dashboard.ts`
- `lib/utils/metrics.ts` (`switchFilter`)

API routes / server helpers:
- `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts`
- `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts`
- `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/analytics.ts` (`getMachineStatsForAnalytics`)

Types:
- `lib/types/index.ts` (`dashboardData`, `DashboardTotals`, `TopPerformingItem`, `TopPerformingData`, `locations`, `ActiveTab`, `ActiveFilters`)
- `lib/types/components/props.ts` (`ChartProps`, `CustomizedLabelProps`, layout props)

Not opened (referenced but outside the audit's prioritization budget): `components/shared/ui/FinancialMetricsCards`, `MachineStatusWidget`, `MapPreview`, `DateFilters`, `DashboardSkeletons`, `PieChartLabelRenderer`, and the `getMetrics`/`fetchTopPerformingData` client helpers behind `switchFilter`. The first-tier verification (typing, auth wrapper, reviewer scale, currency) was completed on the directly-reachable data-fetch chain.

- REACT/NEXT: direct React imports (no React namespace), section comments with `// =====`, skeleton loaders matching exact layout (no spinners), financial color via getFinancialColorClass/getGrossColorClass, useMemo/useCallback.
- HOOK STRUCTURE: 13-section order (External Deps→Types→Helpers→Hook→Store→Local State grouped→Form→Computed→Debounced→Refs→Effects→Handlers grouped→Return).
- FILE ORG / placement: shared/types vs lib/types vs app/api/lib/types; lib/helpers vs app/api/lib/helpers; redundancy/duplication; files >limits.
- SECURITY: location filter on all scoped queries, reviewer scale, session invalidation, conditional cookie secure.
- PERFORMANCE: cursor, N+1, batch queries.
- REDUNDANCY: unused imports/exports/types, duplicate types, dead code, files that should be merged/split.

For each issue found, capture: Category | File (with path) | Issue | Severity (Critical/High/Med/Low) | Concrete Fix.

Return a structured report:
## Dashboard Findings
- A markdown findings table (Category | File | Issue | Severity | Fix).
## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)
## Files in the Dashboard tree (full list of what you read)
Be specific with file paths and line references. Prioritize real, verifiable issues over nitpicks, but DO note convention violations. Do NOT make changes.

Confirmed: `DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` is imported nowhere (only self-match) — it's dead code. I now have everything needed for the report.

## Dashboard Findings

| Category | File | Issue | Severity | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Redundancy / Dead code | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` | Entire component is unused — imported nowhere (grep confirms only self-reference). It still renders a placeholder `"Pie chart would render here"`, has its own duplicate `TopPerformingItem` type, and calls a separate `/api/metrics/top-performing` endpoint the live dashboard doesn't use. | High | Delete the file. If the `/api/metrics/top-performing` route is only used by this dead component + e2e mocks, flag that route for review too. |
| Type Safety | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` (L5-14) | Local `TopPerformingItem` type duplicates and conflicts with `lib/types` `TopPerformingItem`; uses index signature `[key: string]: unknown` for domain data. Also `catch (err: unknown)` annotates the catch param (forbidden). | Med | Removed with the file. If kept, import the shared type and drop the annotation. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L143-145), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L127-129) | `gamingLocations?.some((loc: Record<string, unknown>) => loc.includeJackpot)` casts domain data to `Record<string,unknown>`. The shared `locations` type has no `includeJackpot` field, so the cast hides a type gap. | Med | Add `includeJackpot?: boolean` to the `locations` element type in `lib/types/index.ts` and type the callback param as that element. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L48-61), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L43-56) | `selectedMachine`/`selectedLocation` inline object types are duplicated verbatim across both layouts (and overlap with modal prop types). | Low | Extract a shared `SelectedMachinePreview` / `SelectedLocationPreview` type to `lib/types/components`. |
| Mongoose Typing | `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/analytics.ts` (L230, L302) | Both `Machine.aggregate([...])` calls use no generic (`Machine.aggregate<T>`) and end in `.exec()`. CLAUDE.md mandates `.aggregate<T>()` with types from `shared/types`. (Meters-cursor rule does not apply here since it's `Machine`, and `$lookup` to gaminglocations is acceptable for Machine, not Meters.) | Med | Add explicit aggregate generics typed from shared types; results are bounded (grouped to one doc) so `.exec()` is acceptable but should still be typed. |
| API Route Structure | `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts` | Does NOT use the mandatory `withApiAuth` wrapper — auth is done manually via `getUserFromServer`/`getUserAccessibleLicenceesFromToken`. Other invariants (numbered STEP comments, JSDoc, perf tracking) are present, but wrapper is required by CLAUDE.md §Technical Standards 1. | High | Refactor to `withApiAuth(req, async ({ user, userRoles }) => {...})`. |
| Reviewer Scale (Security) | `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts` | Returns financial totals (`totalDrop`, `totalCancelledCredits`, `totalGross` via `getMachineStatsForAnalytics`) with NO `getReviewerScale` applied. The dashboard machine-status widget only shows counts, but the financial fields leak unscaled. By contrast `locationAggregation` correctly applies `getMoneyInScale`/`getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale`. | High | Apply reviewer scaling to the financial fields before returning, or strip the financial fields from this endpoint's response since the dashboard only consumes counts. |
| Reviewer Scale consistency | `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts` (L390-427) | Uses split `getMoneyInScale`/`getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale` rather than `getReviewerScale` named in CLAUDE.md §5. Likely an intentional newer two-multiplier model, but worth confirming it's the sanctioned helper. | Low | Confirm `reviewerScale.ts` exports these as the canonical path; update CLAUDE.md if so. |
| Currency (CRITICAL rule) | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx` (L657-685) | Y-axis and Tooltip formatters use raw `toLocaleString`/manual `K/M/B/T` formatting with NO currency code — violates "tooltip formatters must also use `formatCurrencyWithCodeString`". Chart shows bare numbers, no `displayCurrency`. | Med | Inject `displayCurrency` via `useCurrencyFormat()` and format the tooltip value with `formatCurrencyWithCodeString`. |
| Gaming Day Offset | `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingCabinetModal.tsx` (L112), `TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx` (L117) | Hardcode gaming-day offset `8` into `getGamingDayRangeForPeriod('Custom', 8, ...)` instead of using the store's `gameDayOffset` (which the store already tracks and defaults to 8, configurable per location). | Med | Pass `gameDayOffset` from `useDashBoardStore()` instead of literal `8`. |
| React/Component Structure | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L71-79), `DashboardTopPerformingSection.tsx` (L48-56) | `NoDataMessage` is a component defined *inside* the render body (re-created every render); duplicated across both files. | Low | Hoist to a shared `components/shared/ui` component. |
| Skeleton rule | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L216-219), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L192-195) | Map-loading state uses an inline ad-hoc `animate-pulse` div rather than a named skeleton in `skeletons/`. Rule requires content-matching named skeletons, no generic pulse blocks. | Low | Extract a `MapPreviewSkeleton`. |
| Type Safety | `DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx` (L193-201), `DashboardMobileLayout.tsx` (L177-185), and 4 `<select onChange>` casts | Repeated inline union literal `'hourly' | 'minute' | 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly'` (the `ChartGranularity` type) re-typed by hand in ~6 places plus `as` casts on select values. | Low | Use the exported `ChartGranularity` type everywhere instead of re-spelling the union. |
| Naming | `lib/types/index.ts` (L14, L86) | Type names `dashboardData` and `locations` are lowercase (should be PascalCase `DashboardData`, `Locations`/`GamingLocations`). Widely used across the dashboard tree. | Low | Rename with codemod; low priority due to blast radius. |
| Dead state / props | `DashboardPageContent.tsx` (L487, L530) | `setRefreshing={() => {}}` passed as no-op to both layouts; layouts never use it meaningfully. `prevTotals` effect (L428-438) updates a ref that is never read. | Low | Remove the unused `setRefreshing` prop and the dead `prevTotals` tracking effect. |
| Performance / complexity | `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx` (809 lines) | Single component does sorting, hourly/weekly/monthly aggregation, gap-filtering, axis formatting — all inline in the render body with no `useMemo`. Heavy O(n) passes recompute every render. Also exceeds reasonable component size. | Med | Extract the aggregation/transform pipeline into a memoized helper (`lib/helpers/dashboard/chartTransform.ts`) and wrap in `useMemo` keyed on `chartData`/`granularity`/`selectedMetrics`. |
| Redundancy | `TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx` (L184-497) | ~300-line inline `fetchData` effect duplicating trend-transform logic that also exists on the location details page (per its own comments "same as location details page"). Should be a shared helper. | Med | Extract to `lib/helpers/locations` shared fetch+transform used by both modal and details page. |
| Error Handling | `lib/utils/metrics.ts` (L81-87) `switchFilter` | Swallows all errors (logs only) including real failures, so the dashboard chart silently shows empty on server errors with no toast. | Low | Distinguish abort vs real error and surface via `showErrorNotification`, mirroring `fetchDashboardTotals`. |
| Dead branch | `DashboardChart.tsx` (L541-547) | `XAxis dataKey` ternary `granularity === 'weekly' || 'monthly' ? 'day' : 'day'` — both branches return `'day'`. | Low | Simplify to `dataKey={isHourlyChart ? 'time' : 'day'}`. |

## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)

1. **Delete dead `DashboardTopPerforming.tsx`** (and assess whether `/api/metrics/top-performing` route is now orphaned — only e2e mocks + this dead file reference it).
2. **Fix `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts`**: wrap in `withApiAuth`, and apply reviewer scaling to (or remove) the financial fields in the response. (Critical: security + structure invariants.)
3. **Add currency formatting to `DashboardChart.tsx`** Y-axis/tooltip via `formatCurrencyWithCodeString` + `displayCurrency`.
4. **Replace hardcoded gaming-day offset `8`** in both preview modals with the store's `gameDayOffset`.
5. **Add aggregate generics** to the two `Machine.aggregate` calls in `analytics.ts`.
6. **Add `includeJackpot?: boolean` to the `locations` type** and drop the `Record<string,unknown>` casts in both layouts.
7. **Extract `DashboardChart` transform pipeline** into a memoized helper; wrap heavy computations in `useMemo`.
8. **Extract the location-trends fetch/transform** out of `TopPerformingLocationModal` into a shared helper.
9. **Hoist shared `NoDataMessage`**, add `MapPreviewSkeleton`, and de-duplicate the `SelectedMachine`/`SelectedLocation` types.
10. **Cleanup**: remove no-op `setRefreshing` props, dead `prevTotals` effect, the `dataKey` no-op ternary; use `ChartGranularity` type instead of inline unions; surface real errors in `switchFilter`.

## Files in the Dashboard tree (read)

Components:
- `app/page.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardPageContent.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardDesktopLayout.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardMobileLayout.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardChart.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/DashboardTopPerforming.tsx` (dead)
- `components/CMS/dashboard/sections/DashboardTopPerformingSection.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingCabinetModal.tsx`
- `components/CMS/dashboard/modals/TopPerformingLocationModal.tsx`

Hooks / store:
- `lib/hooks/data/useDashboardFilters.ts`
- `lib/hooks/data/useDashboardRefresh.ts`
- `lib/store/dashboardStore.ts`

Helpers / utils:
- `lib/helpers/dashboard.ts`
- `lib/utils/metrics.ts` (`switchFilter`)

API routes / server helpers:
- `app/api/analytics/machines/stats/route.ts`
- `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts`
- `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/analytics.ts` (`getMachineStatsForAnalytics`)

Types:
- `lib/types/index.ts` (`dashboardData`, `DashboardTotals`, `TopPerformingItem`, `TopPerformingData`, `locations`, `ActiveTab`, `ActiveFilters`)
- `lib/types/components/props.ts` (`ChartProps`, `CustomizedLabelProps`, layout props)

Not opened (referenced but outside the audit's prioritization budget): `components/shared/ui/FinancialMetricsCards`, `MachineStatusWidget`, `MapPreview`, `DateFilters`, `DashboardSkeletons`, `PieChartLabelRenderer`, and the `getMetrics`/`fetchTopPerformingData` client helpers behind `switchFilter`. The first-tier verification (typing, auth wrapper, reviewer scale, currency) was completed on the directly-reachable data-fetch chain.

content.text:
I now have comprehensive coverage of the Reports tree: all three tabs, their orchestration hooks, navigation hooks, the three API routes, and the key helpers. I have enough to produce a thorough, verifiable audit. Let me compile the report.

## Reports Findings

| Category | File | Issue | Severity | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Performance/Mongoose | `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/meters.ts:327-340` (`fetchMachinesData`) | Licencee filtering is done **in-memory** after fetching ALL machines for the locations, then re-querying `GamingLocations` for the licencee. This is an N+1-ish double query and loads machines that get discarded. Also uses `.lean<GamingMachine[]>()` then immediately re-casts each row through `Record<string, unknown>`. | High | Build the licencee→locationIds set first and add `gamingLocation: { $in: licenceeLocationIds }` to `machineMatchStage` so the DB filters; drop the post-fetch `.filter`. |
| Mongoose typing | `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/meters.ts:233, 362, 466, 586` | Query results typed `.lean<GamingLocationDocument[]>()` / `.lean<GamingMachine[]>()` but the `.map` callbacks annotate params as `Record<string, unknown>` and cast every field (`locationData._id as string`, etc.), defeating the generic. Violates "never cast results after the fact." | Med | Use the `.lean<T>()` generic types directly in the map callbacks; remove `as` casts. |
| Type safety | `app/api/lib/helpers/reports/meters.ts:39-49` | `MachineData.sasMeters?: unknown` for domain data; and a second `MachineData` type exists here that collides conceptually with `shared/types/machines` `MachineData` (re-exported in `shared/types/reports.ts`). Duplicate domain type name. | Med | Type `sasMeters` properly; rename this local type (e.g. `MeterReportMachine`) or source from `shared/types`. |
| Type safety / `any`-equivalent | `app/api/reports/meters/route.ts:321-335`, `locations/route.ts:205-220`, `machines/route.ts:231-244` | `userPayload` repeatedly cast inline via `as { moneyInMultiplier?... }` literal objects in every route. CLAUDE.md: type generics MUST come from `shared/types`, not inline literals; avoid scattered casts. | Med | Define a `ReviewerScalableUser` type in `shared/types` (or `app/api/lib/types`) and type `withApiAuth`'s `user` once. |
| Reviewer scale convention | `app/api/reports/meters/route.ts:336-353` | Reviewer scaling is applied **inline** in the route (manual `item.metersIn * moneyInScale`, manual `netGross` recompute) rather than via a shared helper. CLAUDE.md §5: "Apply… via getReviewerScale… never inline." Duplicated again in `locations` via `buildLocationResults`. | High | Extract a `applyReviewerScaleToMeterRow` helper and reuse; centralize the netGross recompute. |
| Mongoose | `app/api/reports/locations/route.ts:109-116` | `Machine.distinct('gamingLocation', {...})` — `distinct` has no `.lean<T>()` and returns `any[]`; `wowLocs.map(id => String(id))` works but is untyped. Acceptable for distinct, but the surrounding `machineMatch`/`locationMatchStage` use `Record<string, unknown>` for domain query objects. | Low | Acceptable for `distinct`; consider typing the result `string[]`. |
| Performance | `app/api/reports/locations/route.ts:130-157` | `GamingLocations.find(...).lean<LocationDocument[]>()` then `Machine.find(machineMatch).lean<GamingMachine[]>()` load full unbounded sets with `.exec()`/no cursor. For large tenants this loads all machines into memory. CLAUDE.md performance targets / cursor guidance. | Med | For large result sets use `.cursor({batchSize:1000})`; at minimum project only needed fields. |
| API route structure | `app/api/reports/machines/route.ts` | Missing **file-level JSDoc `@module`** tag (other two routes have it). Route is 339 lines (ok) but the inline match-stage building (STEP 2, ~90 lines) exceeds the ">20-30 lines extract to helper" rule and is not extracted. | Med | Add `@module` JSDoc; extract `buildMachinesMatchStages()` to `helpers/reports/machines.ts`. |
| API route structure | `app/api/reports/meters/route.ts:122` | `extractUserFromRequest(req)` is called to get `user`, but `withApiAuth` already provides `userPayload`. Two user sources in one handler is confusing/duplicative. Also step labels are misnumbered: log says "STEP 3" under the STEP 2 banner (lines 128 vs 135), "STEP 9.5", etc. | Low | Use a single user object; fix step-number log strings. |
| Currency convention | `app/api/reports/machines/route.ts:61-62` | `displayCurrency` defaults to `'USD'` and is passed down, but reviewer multipliers are applied in helpers while currency conversion correctness depends on helper internals not visible here — ensure conversion happens AFTER scale per CLAUDE.md. (meters route does scale before convert correctly at 336/404.) | Low | Verify `getMachineStats`/`getOverviewMachines` convert after scaling; document order. |
| Dead/placeholder code | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageContent.tsx:100-102, 186-193` | `const hasAccess = true; const isLoading = false;` are hardcoded constants, making the `if (isLoading)` and `if (!hasAccess)` branches and `ReportsPageAuthLoadingState`/`ReportsPageAccessDeniedState` permanently dead. The `isLoading` is also passed to `PageLayout` disabled and the overlay. | Med | Remove dead branches or wire to real auth/loading state; if intentionally always-true, delete the unreachable states + their components. |
| Dead code | `lib/hooks/data/useReportsTabContent.ts:40-43` | `isTabTransitioning` always returns `false` (with a "could be enhanced" comment) and is returned but never used by `ReportsPageContent`. | Low | Remove `isTabTransitioning` from hook + return type. |
| Redundancy | `lib/hooks/navigation/useReportsNavigation.ts:22-23` | `const availableTabs = reportsTabsConfig;` is a pointless rename; comment "All tabs are available" contradicts the role-filtering already done in `ReportsPageContent`. `isLoading`/`availableTabs` returned but `ReportsPageContent` ignores them. | Low | Inline `reportsTabsConfig`; trim unused returns. |
| Anti-pattern (state sync) | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageContent.tsx:108-115` + every tab | Refresh is driven by a `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('refreshReports'))` with a hardcoded `setTimeout(1000)`; each tab adds a `window.addEventListener`. This is a global-event coupling that bypasses React state and React Query. Fragile and untestable. | Med | Replace with a shared Zustand `refreshNonce` or React Query `invalidateQueries`. |
| State management | `lib/hooks/reports/useMetersTabData.ts` (whole file, 642 lines) | Manual `axios` + `useState` + batch/abort orchestration instead of React Query, which CLAUDE.md §10 mandates ("all API data fetching in components uses useQuery/useMutation"). Manual batch-window pagination, manual abort, manual dedup. Same pattern in machines/locations tabs. | High | Migrate to `useQuery`/`useInfiniteQuery`; removes hundreds of lines of manual cache/abort code. |
| Hook structure | `lib/hooks/reports/useMetersTabData.ts:65` | Hook destructures props but ignores `itemsPerPage`, `pagesPerBatch`, `searchTerm` (only `debouncedSearchTerm` used) — unused params still in the `UseMetersTabDataProps` type. Section ordering also diverges from the mandated 13-section order (Helpers appear after State/Location-Admin logic). | Low | Drop unused props; reorder sections per CLAUDE.md §7. |
| Type safety | `lib/hooks/reports/useMetersTabData.ts:326-365, 482-487` | Repeated `(meterData as Record<string, unknown>)._id`, `(item as Record<string, unknown>).machineDocumentId`, and `(err as Record<string, unknown>)?.response...` chains — domain data cast to `Record<string,unknown>`, explicitly disallowed. | Med | Extend `MetersReportData` type to include `machineDocumentId`/`_id`; use `axios.isAxiosError(err)` for error typing. |
| Type location / duplication | `lib/hooks/reports/useMetersTabData.ts:32-44` | Local `HourlyChartData` and `Location` types are re-declared here AND inside `ReportsMetersTab` (`Location` shape) AND the API helper (`HourlyChartData` from `shared/types/meters`). Triplicated. | Med | Source `Location` and `HourlyChartData` from `shared/types`/`lib/types`. |
| Currency | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsMetersTab.tsx` | Tab reads `displayCurrency` from store and passes to export, but does not call `useCurrencyFormat()`; rendering of currency is delegated to `ReportsMetersTable` (not audited deeply) — confirm it uses `formatCurrencyWithCodeString`. | Low | Verify `ReportsMetersTable` formatting; ensure `?currency=` passed (it is, line 295). |
| Skeleton/loaders | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageLoadingStates.tsx:20-48` & `ReportsMetersTab.tsx:325-353` | `ReportsPageLoadingOverlay` uses a spinning `RefreshCw` icon + "Loading reports…" text — a generic spinner, violating the "no generic spinners, skeletons must match layout" rule. The meters loading state at least renders the table shell. | Med | Replace the overlay spinner with a skeleton, or remove (it's gated by the always-false `isLoading`). |
| Type safety | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageLoadingStates.tsx:13-15` | `Record<string, never>` used for empty prop types and empty destructure `({}: Props)`. Minor; the empty-object-pattern with `Record<string,never>` is borderline but acceptable. | Low | Could simplify to no-prop components. |
| Naming / file placement | `lib/hooks/data/useReportsTabContent.ts` vs `lib/hooks/navigation/useReportsNavigation.ts` | Two closely-related reports-navigation hooks split across `data/` and `navigation/` folders; `useReportsTabContent` is rendering/animation logic (not data). Misfiled under `data/`. | Low | Move `useReportsTabContent` to `lib/hooks/navigation/` or a `ui/` folder. |
| Type safety | `components/CMS/reports/tabs/machines/ReportsMachinesTab.tsx:424-447, 480-491` | `a[topMachinesSortKey as keyof MachineEvaluationData] as number` and `valA as number` casts; sort comparator assumes numeric. `machineToGamingMachine` returns an object cast via `as Parameters<typeof openEditModal>[0]` — large structural cast. | Low | Narrow sort keys to numeric subset; build a proper partial `GamingMachine`. |
| Dead state | `components/CMS/reports/tabs/machines/ReportsMachinesTab.tsx:106, 110` | `topMachinesSortDirection`/`bottomMachinesSortDirection` declared via `useState` but their setters are intentionally omitted, so they're constants — `useState` is misused for a constant. | Low | Replace with plain `const`. |
| Type bloat / redundancy | `shared/types/reports.ts` | This file holds many types with **no consumer in the Reports tree** (`ScheduledReport`, `CustomerMetrics`, `VoucherMetrics`, `ComplianceMetrics`, `DashboardWidget`, `UserReportPreferences`, `ReportGenerationStatus`, `PerformanceComparison`, `RealTimeMetrics`, `LogisticsEntry`, `ReportConfig`, `ReportData`). Likely dead/aspirational types. | Med | grep usages; delete unused exports or move to a `future/` types file. |
| Type file convention | `shared/types/reports.ts:288-304` | `LocationTrendPoint` uses an index signature `[locationId: string]: {…} | string | undefined` mixing a `day`/`time` string with object values — loose typing for domain data; forces casts downstream. | Low | Model as `{ day: string; time?: string; locations: Record<string, TrendMetrics> }`. |
| Gaming day | `components/CMS/reports/ReportsDateFilters.tsx:135-191` | `handleFilterClick` builds Today/Yesterday/7d/30d ranges with **plain local midnight/`23:59:59`**, ignoring the 8AM-8AM Trinidad gaming-day offset. The offset is applied server-side, but the client `selectedDateRange` shown via `DateRangeIndicator` won't match gaming-day semantics. Also handles legacy `'last7days'`/`'last30days'` values not in the button list. | Med | Use `lib/utils/gamingDayRange.ts` to derive the displayed range; drop dead `last7days`/`last30days` cases. |
| Consistency | `app/api/reports/meters/route.ts` (no try/catch) vs `locations`/`machines` (full try/catch + `logRouteError`) | The meters route has **no top-level try/catch** and no `logRouteError`, unlike the other two routes. An unhandled throw (e.g. `parseMetersReportParams` throws on missing `locations`) becomes a 500 with no structured log. | Med | Wrap handler body in try/catch with `logRouteError`, consistent with sibling routes. |
| Slow-threshold inconsistency | `meters/route.ts:427` (`>2000`), CLAUDE.md says `>1000` | Routes warn at `>2000ms`; CLAUDE.md §1 mandates warn at `>1000ms`. | Low | Standardize the slow-warning threshold to 1000ms. |

## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)

1. **Push licencee filtering into the DB query** in `fetchMachinesData` (`meters.ts`) — biggest correctness/perf win; eliminates loading-then-discarding machines.
2. **Migrate the three tab data hooks to React Query** (`useMetersTabData`, `useMachinesTabData`, `useLocationsTabData`) — removes the manual axios/abort/batch/dedup machinery and the `window` custom-event refresh hack, satisfying CLAUDE.md §10. Largest line reduction.
3. **Centralize reviewer scaling** into a shared helper and apply in meters/locations/machines uniformly (remove the inline `item.metersIn * moneyInScale` block in `meters/route.ts`).
4. **Define a typed `withApiAuth` user** (`ReviewerScalableUser` in shared/api types) and delete the repeated inline `as {moneyInMultiplier…}` casts across all three routes.
5. **Remove dead code**: hardcoded `hasAccess`/`isLoading` + unreachable states in `ReportsPageContent`; `isTabTransitioning`; `availableTabs` alias and unused returns in the nav hooks; `useState` constants in machines tab.
6. **Eliminate `Record<string,unknown>` casts** in `meters.ts` map callbacks and `useMetersTabData` by using the `.lean<T>()` generics and extending `MetersReportData` with `machineDocumentId`/`_id`; use `axios.isAxiosError`.
7. **Add try/catch + `logRouteError`** to the meters route; add the missing `@module` JSDoc to the machines route; fix mislabeled STEP log strings; standardize the 1000ms slow threshold.
8. **De-duplicate types**: consolidate `Location`/`HourlyChartData`/`MachineData` into `shared/types`; prune the large block of unused types in `shared/types/reports.ts` after grep confirmation.
9. **Apply gaming-day offset to client-side displayed date ranges** in `ReportsDateFilters` and drop the dead `last7days`/`last30days` cases.
10. **Replace the loading-overlay spinner** with a skeleton (or remove with the dead `isLoading`).

## Files in the Reports tree (read in full)

Entry / shell:
- `app/reports/page.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageContent.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/ReportsNavigation.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/ReportsDateFilters.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/ReportsPageLoadingStates.tsx`

Tabs:
- `components/CMS/reports/tabs/meters/ReportsMetersTab.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/tabs/machines/ReportsMachinesTab.tsx`
- `components/CMS/reports/tabs/locations/ReportsLocationsTab.tsx`

Hooks:
- `lib/hooks/navigation/useReportsNavigation.ts`
- `lib/hooks/data/useReportsTabContent.ts`
- `lib/hooks/reports/useMetersTabData.ts`

API routes:
